summary: Built-in example development integration profiles for agent-os. These profiles describe git and review posture only; session instructions come from repo DOX and direct user intent.
override_policy: A direct user instruction for the current repo or session overrides the active profile unless it would discard work, publish externally, or perform a destructive operation.

axes.session_identity: The current coding session and its explicit user/task context; AOS core does not define project-agent personas.
axes.execution_surface: The concrete place work happens, such as the current checkout, a short-lived branch, or a pull request.
axes.capability_route: Path, tool, and test routing mechanics for the current task.
axes.workflow_profile: Low-level development integration policy: branch, commit, review, PR, merge authority, and release posture.
axes.handoff_boundary: The boundary that must be satisfied before work is integrated, published, or delegated.

profiles.name: Remote Branch Relay
intended_for: single developer with an occasional remote reviewer
local implementation coordinated with a GitHub-visible review authority
workflows where a pushed branch is the durable handoff artifact between local and remote sessions
profiles.main_role: stable integration target; implementation work lands only after the designated review authority accepts it
profiles.review_default: designated review authority reads the remote branch and merges or flags; the authority can be remote, human, or local
profiles.commit_strategy: scoped commits on a topic/<slug> branch; push branch to origin at completion when explicitly authorized
profiles.branch_strategy: one branch per work slice; named topic/<slug>; branch is the remote-visible relay artifact and is deleted after merge
branch_required_for: implementation slice that needs remote review
branch_optional_for: docs-only or config-only changes that carry no implementation risk
profiles.review_authority: designated review authority with GitHub repository access; often remote and without local checkout
session_guidance: Create a branch named topic/<slug> from main before starting implementation work that needs remote review.
Commit verified work to that branch and push it to origin at completion only when the profile and dispatch explicitly authorize publication.
Report the branch name, HEAD SHA, verification, and local-only state in the completion report. Do not merge to main.
The review authority reads the remote branch, evaluates the work, and merges to main or requests changes.
When the review authority is remote, it evaluates GitHub-visible evidence and may request a local probe for facts it cannot observe.

profiles.name: Local Checkpoint
intended_for: single developer working in one local checkout
workflows that need branches, scoped commits, and stashes without linked git worktrees or automatic pushes
local review loops where GitHub publication remains an explicit later decision
profiles.default_work_surface: single checkout at /Users/Michael/Code/agent-os on the current local branch
profiles.main_role: stable integration target; the human decides when local branch work is merged or published
profiles.review_default: human or local review reads local commits and verification before merge or push; a short-lived PR can be created when outsider review would be useful
profiles.commit_strategy: small scoped local commits on the current branch or one local topic branch; no automatic push
profiles.branch_strategy: use the single repo checkout; create or switch local branches only when the user or work card names them; never create linked git worktrees
branch_required_for: dirty baseline that must be preserved
work that needs a local review checkpoint before merge
branch_optional_for: direct local loop on the current branch
outsider review checkpoint after local acceptance
profiles.review_authority: human or local reviewer using local branch state; GitHub publication is explicit, not automatic
profiles.branch_naming_convention: topic/<slug> when a branch is warranted
session_guidance: Work only in /Users/Michael/Code/agent-os; do not create linked git worktrees or put repo-mode binaries outside the main checkout.
Use branches, scoped commits, and named stashes to preserve unrelated work before switching context.
Commit locally when the work is finished or the user asks for a checkpoint; do not push, open PRs, merge, or clean branches unless explicitly assigned.
Report local branch state, verification, and stashes before recommending merge, push, PR, or cleanup.
When the next step crosses a non-local gate, stop with an actionable gate naming the blocked action, recommended default, exact approval phrase, command path, and safe alternative.
At practical review points, suggest a small focused PR only as an explicit human-approved option.
The repo-mode ./aos binary is stable infrastructure at /Users/Michael/Code/agent-os/aos and should not be rebuilt except for explicitly accepted native changes.

profiles.name: Hybrid Trunk
intended_for: local or human review without mandatory pull requests
profiles.review_default: local tests, focused review, or human review when risk warrants it
profiles.commit_strategy: small frequent scoped commits directly on main
profiles.branch_strategy: use short-lived branches sparingly as a safety valve
branch_required_for: external collaboration requiring a pull request
session_guidance: Default to main for small, reversible changes.
Create a branch when risk, duration, or collaboration calls for it.

profiles.name: GitHub Flow
profiles.branch_strategy: one short-lived branch per feature, bugfix, refactor, or docs change
branch_optional_for: maintainer emergency patch when policy allows it
session_guidance: Keep branches short-lived and merge through pull requests.

profiles.name: GitFlow
profiles.review_default: pull request review into the appropriate long-lived branch
profiles.branch_strategy: develop for integration, feature branches for work, release branches for release preparation, hotfix branches from main
branch_optional_for: small docs correction on a release branch when policy allows it
session_guidance: Respect long-lived environment and release branches.
